Eucalyptus baueri, also known as the Broad-leaved Peppermint or Broad-leaved Mallee Eucalyptus, is a tree species native to Australia. The leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues such as colds and flu due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Eucalyptus baueri include volatile oils, flavonoids, and tannins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Eucalyptus baueri is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, essential oils, and cough drops to alleviate respiratory issues and provide relief from congestion.
